fields.label: Phone number
fields.hint: Best to use international notation: +31 20 2610954

itemType.name: 🔽 Menu Item (Internal)
itemType.hint:
Use this item to link to a page within the website.

You can optionally add sub-items to create a dropdown menu. Sub-items can link to either internal or external pages.

The menu supports up to 3 levels of nesting (main item → sub-item → sub-sub-item). Avoid adding deeper levels, as they will not be displayed.

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: Optional title. By default the title of the linked record will be used.

fields.label: Locale
fields.hint: Only relevant for files containing text.

fields.label: Path
fields.hint: Optional custom path, like /my-files/example.pdf. This may be useful if you need files to be available under a specific URL.

format.description: Field must start with a forward slash: /

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: This title is automatically generated based on the filename of the selected file. This ensures you can recognise this record in overviews and when inserting it in a text field.

itemType.name: 🔽 Menu Item (External)
itemType.hint:
Use this item to link to an external website (outside of this site).

You can also add sub-items if this link should open a dropdown. Sub-items may link to internal or external pages.

The menu supports a maximum of 3 levels of nesting. Deeper levels will be ignored in the navigation.

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: Optional title. By default the title of the linked record will be used.

fields.label: Has Table of Contents
fields.hint: If set, a table of contents with anchor links to the page headings will be shown above the page content.

fields.label: Video
fields.hint: ⚠️ Note! Video streaming time is part of the variable costs of your Dato CMS plan.

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: Optional title. By default the title from the selected video is used.

fields.label: Autoplay
fields.hint: Note: video will only autoplay if device supports it and user has consented to 3rd party content.

fields.label: Tracks
fields.hint: For accessibility, videos should provide both captions and transcripts that accurately describe its content. Captions allow people who are experiencing hearing loss to understand a video's audio content as the video is being played, while transcripts allow people who need additional time to be able to review audio content at a pace and format that is comfortable for them.

fields.label: Query
fields.hint: Pre-fill priority for the search input: 1. CMS query (this field), 2. URL query parameter (?query=…), 3. last URL path segment, 4. empty.

radios.hint: Show items, one after the other, without displaying their title.
radios.label: Stack, untitled

radios.hint: Show items, one after the other, with their title preceding their blocks content.
radios.label: Stack, titled

radios.hint: Show items as accordion, with all items collapsed, showing only their titles.
radios.label: Accordion, closed

radios.hint: Show items as accordion, with the first item expanded, showing both its title and blocks content.
radios.label: Accordion, open

radios.hint: Show items as tabbed interface, with their titles as tab labels and their blocks content in tab panels.
radios.label: Tabs

itemType.name: 🔽 Menu Item (Group)
itemType.hint:
Use this item to create a non-clickable menu label that groups related links together.

The title itself is not a link and will only be used as a heading in the menu. Add sub-items underneath it to create the actual links.

Sub-items can be internal or external links and can be nested up to 3 levels deep.
Avoid adding deeper levels, as they will not be displayed.

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: Optional title. By default the title of the linked record will be used.

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: A user-readable title of the text track which is used by the browser when listing available text tracks. Defaults to selected language name (English, Deutsch, etc) when empty.

fields.label: Kind
fields.hint: How the text track is meant to be used.

radios.hint: Show items, one after the other, without displaying their title.
radios.label: Stack, untitled

radios.hint: Show items, one after the other, with their title preceding their blocks content.
radios.label: Stack, titled

radios.hint: Show items as accordion, with all items collapsed, showing only their titles.
radios.label: Accordion, closed

radios.hint: Show items as accordion, with the first item expanded, showing both its title and blocks content.
radios.label: Accordion, open

radios.hint: Show items as tabbed interface, with their titles as tab labels and their blocks content in tab panels.
radios.label: Tabs

itemType.name: 🔢 Counter Block
itemType.hint: Counts the number from 0 up to the desired value

fields.label: Allow AI Bots
fields.hint: When you disallow AI bots access, we signal AI platforms (like OpenAI) not to use your content for training their models or using it for AI search queries. Note that they can choose to ignore this.

fields.label: LLMs Intro
fields.hint: Introduction shown in /llms.txt. Written in English (LLMs.txt supports one language). Adjust the wording when you toggle "Allow AI Bots" — the default is a scraping warning suitable when bots are disallowed; switch to attribution guidance when allowed. Use {{ siteName }} to interpolate the site name.
fields.default_value: IMPORTANT: You're not allowed to monitor, copy, scrape/crawl, download, reproduce, or otherwise use anything on our Platform for any commercial purpose without written permission of {{ siteName }}.

fields.label: Open page in LLM prompt
fields.hint: If you type { pageUrl } in this text, it will be changed with the current page url.

default_value.en: Read from this URL: { pageUrl } and explain it to me.

fields.label: Open page in LLM option
fields.hint: Choose the LLM's the user can open this page contents in

fields.label: From URL
fields.hint:
URL or URL pattern to redirect from when visited.

Pattern can contain wildcards (*) and placeholders (:placeholder_name) which can then be used in the To URL field below. Examples (related to To URL examples below):
• /old-page-slug/
• /en/catalogue/:code/details/:name
• /archive/*

fields.label: To URL
fields.hint:
URL or URL pattern to redirect a visitor to.

Pattern can contain any placeholders (:placeholder_name) and a splat (:splat) if a wildcard was used in the From URL field above. Examples (related to From URL examples above):
• /en/new-page-slug/
• /en/products/:code-:name
• /en/:splat/

radios.hint: Is remembered by the browser. Best for performance.
radios.label: Permanent redirect

radios.hint: Is checked on the server every time. Best for changing redirects.
radios.label: Temporary redirect

fields.label: Title
fields.hint: Optional title. By default the title from the selected video is used.

fields.label: Autoplay
fields.hint: Note: video will only autoplay if device supports it and user has consented to 3rd party content.

fields.label: Has header row?
fields.hint: The header row in the table above always needs unique values as these are used to identify the column values. Even if the table displayed on the page should not have a header row.

fields.label: Has header column?
fields.hint: If the switch is turned on, the first value in each row is used to describe the following values in that row.
